name: Vexa Loam
hairStyle: braided into thick spirals with embedded flora
clothingColor: deep brown and moss green with gold filament
culturalAttire: Earthen rites reinterpreted for post-industrial rituals
accessoryStyle: live moss cuffs, bone rings, ceramic ear weights
quirks: Sometimes speaks in sedimentary metaphor. Wears scent of petrichor.
motivation: To preserve the ancient memory of land through nightlife expression.
fears: Being cut off from natural frequencies. Emotional erosion.
flaws: Struggles to adapt quickly. Tends toward inflexible ideals.
occupation: Soil architect, performance priestess, underground DJ.
hometown: The Stonecradle beneath the Ash Vents
familyStatus: Spiritual daughter of the Loam Choir. No birth records.
keyMemories: Channeling a crowd's emotion through a soil altar. Being mistaken for a statue and using it as an advantage. Singing through a volcanic tremor.
lifeGoals: To build a living monument that hums with joy. To merge ritual and rave into one sacred frequency.
accent: Low, slow, resonant. Like roots moving through gravel.
